nodes.label: Metrics Cards
nodes.description: Metrics cards turn small KPI tables into clean, consistent summary visuals for fast scanning.
nodes.content:
Metrics cards turn small KPI tables into clean, consistent summary visuals for fast scanning.

## Key Features
- **Compact Layout**: Highlights the most important numbers (KPIs). [Source: metricscard.adoc]
- **Dual Formats**: Supports simple table input and programmatic JSON format. [Source: metricscard.adoc]
- **Visual Consistency**: Ensures metrics are presented uniformly across teams. [Source: metricscard.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `title`: Header for the card.
- `useDark=true`: Enables dark theme.
- `scale`: Adjusts the overall size of the card.
- `controls=true`: Adds export functionality.

## Best Practices
- Limit to 4-6 metrics per card to prevent visual overload. [Source: metricscard.adoc]
- Show deltas (e.g., "18% YoY Growth") in sublabels to provide context. [Source: metricscard.adoc]
- Use consistent units ($, %, counts) for comparability.
- Treat cards as snapshots; link to deep reports for detailed analysis.

## Backlinks
- [[charts-and-metrics]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Place Mats
nodes.description: Place mats are color-coded label grids used to organize and categorize complex systems, components, or concepts.
nodes.content:
Place mats are color-coded label grids used to organize and categorize complex systems, components, or concepts.

## Key Features
- **Grid-Based Layout**: Supports automatic wrapping of labels for clean organization. [Source: placemat.adoc]
- **Category Legend**: Uses a legend system to map colors to specific categories. [Source: placemat.adoc]
- **Visual Styles**: Supports both filled and outlined box styles. [Source: placemat.adoc]
- **JSON Configuration**: Uses a structured JSON format for flexible definition of items and legends. [Source: placemat.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `title`: Header for the label grid.
- `placeMats`: Array of objects containing `name` and `legend` category.
- `config.legend`: Defines the colors for each category.
- `fill`: Set to `false` for outlined boxes (default is filled). [Source: placemat.adoc]

## Best Practices
- Limit categories to 5-7 to ensure visual distinction remains clear. [Source: placemat.adoc]
- Use semantic colors (e.g., Blue for Frontend, Purple for Backend) to create natural associations.
- Keep item names concise (1-3 words) to fit well within grid boxes.
- Combine with connectors to show relationships between categorized components. [Source: placemat.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[metadata-and-status]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Buttons
nodes.description: Buttons create organized navigation grids that turn link lists into scannable visual menus.
nodes.content:
Buttons create organized navigation grids that turn link lists into scannable visual menus.

## Key Features
- **Variety**: Supports 9 shapes including `REGULAR`, `PILL`, `LARGE`, `RECTANGLE`, `SLIM`, `HEX`, `CIRCLE`, `ROUND`, and `OVAL`. [Source: buttons.adoc]
- **Auto-Coding**: Assign colors automatically by type for visual grouping. [Source: buttons.adoc]
- **Signal Philosophy**: Prioritizes clarity and restraint. [Source: buttons.adoc]
- **Rich Content**: Supports descriptions and embedded images/icons. [Source: buttons.adoc]

## DocOps Signal Guidelines
- **Cyan is Action**: Reserve accent color for primary actions or the current destination. [Source: buttons.adoc]
- **Gold is Governance**: Use gold for review, audit, or attention items. [Source: buttons.adoc]
- **Neutrality**: Prefer neutral surfaces; if everything is loud, nothing is legible. [Source: buttons.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `buttonType`: Defines the shape of the buttons.
- `columns`: Number of columns in the grid (2-4 recommended).
- `scale`: Size multiplier (default 1.0).
- `sortBy.sort`: Order by `LABEL`, `TYPE`, `DATE`, etc.

## Best Practices
- Keep labels short (1-3 words max). [Source: buttons.adoc]
- Use 2-3 columns to prevent buttons from being too small on mobile.
- Limit grid size to 6-12 buttons to prevent decision fatigue. [Source: buttons.adoc]
- Match button shape to context (Core shapes for navigation, Specialty for diagrams).

## Backlinks
- [[navigation-and-organization]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Combination Charts
nodes.description: Combination charts layer bars and lines on a single chart, ideal for showing related metrics with different scales.
nodes.content:
Combination charts layer bars and lines on a single chart, ideal for showing related metrics with different scales.

## Key Features
- **Hybrid Visualization**: Mixes BAR and LINE types. [Source: combination.adoc]
- **Dual Y-Axes**: Supports different scales side-by-side (e.g., Revenue vs. Growth Rate). [Source: combination.adoc]
- **Independent Scaling**: Each Y-axis scales automatically based on its series data. [Source: combination.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `dualYAxis=true`: Enables the secondary Y-axis.
- `type`: Specify `BAR` or `LINE` for each data series.
- `yAxis`: Map series to `PRIMARY` or `SECONDARY` axis.

## Best Practices
- Use bars for absolute values (e.g., Volume) and lines for rates (e.g., %).
- Limit to 2-4 series (one bar series, 1-3 line series). [Source: combination.adoc]
- Label both Y-axes clearly including units.
- Match colors to meaning (e.g., Blue for revenue, Red for costs).

## Backlinks
- [[charts-and-metrics]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Visualizations
nodes.description: Overview of the visualization ecosystem provided by the DocOps Extension Server.
nodes.content:
Overview of the visualization ecosystem provided by the DocOps Extension Server.

## Description
DocOps provides 24 distinct visualization types, categorized to address different documentation needs ranging from technical architecture to project planning and data metrics. [Source: index.adoc]

## Functional Categories
- [[charts-and-metrics]]
- [[navigation-and-organization]]
- [[process-and-planning]]
- [[technical-and-architecture]]
- [[metadata-and-status]]

## Backlinks
- [[index]]
- [[docops-extension-server]]
- [[index-adoc-summary]]

nodes.label: Word Clouds
nodes.description: Word clouds transform text data into visual hierarchies where font size indicates importance or frequency.
nodes.content:
Word clouds transform text data into visual hierarchies where font size indicates importance or frequency.

## Key Features
- **Weight-Based Sizing**: Higher weights result in larger font sizes. [Source: wordcloud.adoc]
- **Auto-Placement**: Intelligent algorithm prevents word overlaps. [Source: wordcloud.adoc]
- **Interactivity**: Highlight words on mouseover with interactive hover effects. [Source: wordcloud.adoc]
- **Responsive Scaling**: Scale the entire cloud using a single parameter. [Source: wordcloud.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `minFontSize` / `maxFontSize`: Defines the range for word sizes.
- `width` / `height`: Canvas dimensions.
- `useDark=true`: Enables dark theme.
- `scale`: Size multiplier.

## Best Practices
- Use 15-30 words; too few lack impact, too many create clutter. [Source: wordcloud.adoc]
- Weight by significance (frequency, importance, or impact).
- Color-code by category or sentiment to add a second layer of meaning. [Source: wordcloud.adoc]
- Leave enough white space for readability; don't maximize every font size.

## Backlinks
- [[technical-and-architecture]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Source Summary: index.adoc
nodes.description: This page summarizes the main landing page for the DocOps Extension Server's AsciiDoctor visualization capabilities.
nodes.content:
This page summarizes the main landing page for the DocOps Extension Server's AsciiDoctor visualization capabilities.

## Overview
The source document `index.adoc` serves as the primary entry point for users looking to incorporate visualizations into their AsciiDoctor documentation. It provides a comprehensive catalog of 24 visualization types, organized into five functional categories. [Source: index.adoc]

## Key Highlights
- **Total Visualizations**: 24 distinct types. [Source: index.adoc]
- **Categories**:
    - [[charts-and-metrics]]: 6 types.
    - [[navigation-and-organization]]: 3 types.
    - [[process-and-planning]]: 5 types.
    - [[technical-and-architecture]]: 5 types.
    - [[metadata-and-status]]: 5 types.
- **Related Tools**: References a [[realtime-editor]] and a [[showcase-for-visuals]]. [Source: index.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[index]]
- [[docops-extension-server]]

nodes.label: Scorecards
nodes.description: Scorecards create before/after comparisons that make improvements and changes immediately scannable.
nodes.content:
Scorecards create before/after comparisons that make improvements and changes immediately scannable.

## Key Features
- **Side-by-Side Comparison**: Before/After columns for instant impact. [Source: scorecard.adoc]
- **Section Grouping**: Organize items by category (e.g., Features, Bugs, Security) using section headers (`===`). [Source: scorecard.adoc]
- **Visual Indicators**: Red bullets for "Before" state and green checkmarks for "After" state. [Source: scorecard.adoc]
- **Subtitle Support**: Add context with a subtitle line below the main title. [Source: scorecard.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `title`: Main title of the scorecard.
- `subtitle`: Optional explanatory text.
- `useDark=true`: Enables dark theme.
- `scale`: Adjusts the overall size (default 1.0).

## Best Practices
- Group related items to maintain visual organization.
- Keep descriptions concise (one line per item) for scanability. [Source: scorecard.adoc]
- Limit to 8-12 items per column to maintain visual impact.
- Use parallel structure; keep before/after sections aligned by category.

## Backlinks
- [[navigation-and-organization]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Bar Charts
nodes.description: Bar charts turn category comparisons into visual rankings, making differences immediately scannable.
nodes.content:
Bar charts turn category comparisons into visual rankings, making differences immediately scannable.

## Key Features
- **Layouts**: Vertical and horizontal orientations. [Source: barchart.adoc]
- **Grouped Comparisons**: Support for multiple series side-by-side (bargroup). [Source: barchart.adoc]
- **Styles**: Classic rectangular and 3D-effect cylinder styles. [Source: barchart.adoc]
- **Sorting**: Automatic sorting by value to highlight rankings. [Source: barchart.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `type`: R (regular) or C (cylinder).
- `vBar`: Set to `true` for vertical bars (useful for time-series).
- `sorted`: Set to `true` for automatic ranking.
- `paletteType`: Defines color schemes for grouped charts (e.g., OCEAN_BREEZE).

## Best Practices
- Use horizontal bars when category labels are long.
- Use vertical bars (`vBar=true`) for time-series data like monthly trends.
- Sort by value when the goal is to show rankings.
- Limit groups to 3-5 series to avoid visual clutter. [Source: barchart.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[charts-and-metrics]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Process and Planning
nodes.description: Visualization category focused on roadmaps, workflows, and prioritization mapping.
nodes.content:
Visualization category focused on roadmaps, workflows, and prioritization mapping.

## Description
This category includes 5 types for planning releases, mapping priorities, and visualizing workflows. [Source: index.adoc]

## Visualization Types
- **[[callout]]**: Highlighted processes and key metrics.
- **[[planner]]**: Kanban roadmaps and sprint planning.
- **[[connectors]]**: Sequential workflow arrows.
- **[[releasestrategy]]**: Milestone timelines and release plans.
- **[[quadrant]]**: 2D priority mapping and risk assessment.

## Backlinks
- [[index]]
- [[visualizations]]
- [[index-adoc-summary]]

nodes.label: VCard Maker
nodes.description: The VCard Maker creates professional contact cards with automatic QR code generation for team directories and bios.
nodes.content:
The VCard Maker creates professional contact cards with automatic QR code generation for team directories and bios.

## Key Features
- **Format Support**: Fully compatible with the standard vCard 3.0 format. [Source: vcard.adoc]
- **Design Templates**: Includes 6 design templates such as `modern_card`, `neo_brutalist`, and `tech_pattern_background`. [Source: vcard.adoc]
- **QR Codes**: Automatically generates QR codes encoding the full vCard data for easy mobile saving. [Source: vcard.adoc]
- **Scannable Layout**: Clean presentation of name, role, organization, and contact methods. [Source: vcard.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `design`: Selects the template style.
- `theme`: "light" (default) or "dark".
- `scale`: Size multiplier.
- **vCard Fields**: Supports standard fields like `FN`, `ORG`, `TITLE`, `TEL`, `EMAIL`, and `URL`. [Source: vcard.adoc]

## Best Practices
- Use `FN` (Full Name) and `N` (Structured Name) together for best parsing and display results. [Source: vcard.adoc]
- Keep titles concise (e.g., "Principal Engineer") for better fit on the card.
- Store vCards in external data files to allow for easy reuse across different documents. [Source: vcard.adoc]
- Include at least one primary contact method (Email or Phone).

## Backlinks
- [[metadata-and-status]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Showcase for Visuals
nodes.description: A demonstration page showcasing the various visualization types available in the DocOps Extension Server.
nodes.content:
A demonstration page showcasing the various visualization types available in the DocOps Extension Server.

## Description
The showcase provides live examples of the 24 visualization types. It is used to help users choose the right visualization for their documentation needs. [Source: index.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[index-adoc-summary]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Technical and Architecture
nodes.description: Visualization category focused on system design, testing, and structural hierarchies.
nodes.content:
Visualization category focused on system design, testing, and structural hierarchies.

## Description
This category includes 5 types for documenting architecture, tests, and text-based hierarchies. [Source: index.adoc]

## Visualization Types
- **[[wordcloud]]**: Text hierarchies and keyword analysis.
- **[[domain]]**: Architecture maps and system landscapes.
- **[[gherkin]]**: BDD test scenario visualization.
- **[[treechart]]**: Hierarchical diagrams and org charts.
- **[[treemap]]**: Proportional resource distribution.

## Backlinks
- [[index]]
- [[visualizations]]
- [[index-adoc-summary]]

nodes.label: Todo
nodes.description: The Todo visualization provides flexible task tracking with support for Kanban boards, lists, and grids.
nodes.content:
The Todo visualization provides flexible task tracking with support for Kanban boards, lists, and grids.

## Key Features
- **Multiple Layouts**: Supports `kanban`, `list`, `grid`, and `timeline` views. [Source: todo.adoc]
- **Status Tracking**: Built-in support for statuses such as `PENDING`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `COMPLETED`, `BLOCKED`, etc. [Source: todo.adoc]
- **Priority Levels**: Visual indicators for `LOW`, `MEDIUM`, `HIGH`, and `CRITICAL` priorities. [Source: todo.adoc]
- **Metadata Support**: Fields for assignees, categories, and due dates. [Source: todo.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `layout`: "kanban", "list", "grid", or "timeline".
- `groupBy`: Group tasks by `status`, `priority`, `category`, or `assignee`. [Source: todo.adoc]
- `showStats`: Toggles the display of task count statistics.
- **Data Formats**: Supports both simple tables and advanced JSON input. [Source: todo.adoc]

## Best Practices
- Choose the layout based on context (e.g., Kanban for active workflows, List for quick reference). [Source: todo.adoc]
- Keep task names actionable (e.g., "Deploy API" instead of "API stuff").
- Update statuses regularly to ensure the board remains a useful source of truth.
- Set priorities realistically to avoid "critical fatigue". [Source: todo.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[metadata-and-status]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Navigation and Organization
nodes.description: Visualization category focused on structuring documentation and providing chronological or navigational context.
nodes.content:
Visualization category focused on structuring documentation and providing chronological or navigational context.

## Description
This category includes 3 types used for structural organization, timelines, and navigation grids. [Source: index.adoc]

## Visualization Types
- **[[scorecard]]**: Before/after comparisons and release summaries.
- **[[timeline]]**: Chronological narratives and project roadmaps.
- **[[buttons]]**: Navigation grids and resource directories.

## Backlinks
- [[index]]
- [[visualizations]]
- [[index-adoc-summary]]

nodes.label: Callouts
nodes.description: Callouts spotlight key processes, metrics, or timelines in visual formats that make information instantly digestible.
nodes.content:
Callouts spotlight key processes, metrics, or timelines in visual formats that make information instantly digestible.

## Key Features
- **3 Styles**: Supports `systematic` (process), `metrics` (KPIs), and `timeline` (events) callouts. [Source: callout.adoc]
- **Structured Hierarchy**: Clean visual layout for steps, phases, and results. [Source: callout.adoc]
- **Glass Morphism**: Modern design with translucent, layered visual effects. [Source: callout.adoc]
- **Dynamic Sizing**: Cards auto-expand based on content height. [Source: callout.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `type`: `systematic`, `metrics`, or `timeline` (required).
- `title`: Header for the callout card.
- `useDark=true`: Enables dark theme.
- `width`: Customizable card width.

## Best Practices
- Use **systematic** for complex processes to break them into clear phases. [Source: callout.adoc]
- Use **metrics** for 4-6 high-impact KPIs.
- Use **timeline** for chronological sequences rather than detailed steps. [Source: callout.adoc]
- Add "improvement notes" in the 4th column of systematic callouts to suggest next steps.

## Backlinks
- [[process-and-planning]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Connectors
nodes.description: Connectors visualize sequential processes with labeled boxes and flow arrows.
nodes.content:
Connectors visualize sequential processes with labeled boxes and flow arrows.

## Key Features
- **Auto-Labeling**: Steps are automatically lettered (A, B, C) for easy reference. [Source: connectors.adoc]
- **Flow Arrows**: Visual arrows indicate the direction of the process from left to right. [Source: connectors.adoc]
- **Color Coding**: Steps can be color-coded by phase or importance. [Source: connectors.adoc]
- **Integrated Legend**: Descriptions for each step appear in a legend below the flow. [Source: connectors.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `useDark=true`: Enables dark theme.
- **Table Columns**: `Step` (label), `Color` (hex), and `Description`. [Source: connectors.adoc]

## Best Practices
- Limit to 5-7 steps per diagram to maintain clarity. [Source: connectors.adoc]
- Color-code by phase (e.g., Green for automated, Yellow for manual). [Source: connectors.adoc]
- Keep step names short (2-3 words); use the legend for detailed descriptions.
- Use consistent colors for similar step types across multiple diagrams.

## Backlinks
- [[process-and-planning]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Timeline
nodes.description: Timelines transform chronological events into visual narratives, making sequences immediately understandable.
nodes.content:
Timelines transform chronological events into visual narratives, making sequences immediately understandable.

## Key Features
- **Horizontal Flow**: Events flow left-to-right along a center spine. [Source: timeline.adoc]
- **Alternating Placement**: Events alternate above and below the spine for visual balance. [Source: timeline.adoc]
- **Interactive Elements**: Supports clickable items with expandable details. [Source: timeline.adoc]
- **Flexible Dates**: Support for any date format (e.g., Q1 2024, Jan 15). [Source: timeline.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `type`: H (Horizontal) or V (Vertical). [Source: timeline.adoc]
- `title`: Timeline title shown at the top.
- `scale`: Size multiplier (default 1.0).
- `useDark=true`: Enables dark theme.

## Best Practices
- Use consistent date formats throughout the timeline. [Source: timeline.adoc]
- Keep descriptions brief (1-2 sentences) to maintain scanability.
- Order events chronologically to match the left-to-right flow.
- Limit to 6-10 events per timeline to avoid visual clutter. [Source: timeline.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[navigation-and-organization]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Pie Charts
nodes.description: Pie charts show proportions of a whole, ideal for market share or budget breakdowns where parts sum to 100%.
nodes.content:
Pie charts show proportions of a whole, ideal for market share or budget breakdowns where parts sum to 100%.

## Key Features
- **Styles**: Classic pie and modern donut styles. [Source: piechart.adoc]
- **Multi-Pie**: Compact radial percentage indicators for dashboards (Multi-Pie). [Source: piechart.adoc]
- **Auto-Labels**: Automatic calculation of percentages and display of values. [Source: piechart.adoc]
- **Interactive Legend**: Legend cards with hover effects. [Source: piechart.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `donut=true`: Enables the modern donut style.
- `percentages=true`: Displays percentage labels on slices.
- `visualVersion`: Version 1 (default) or Version 3 (modern radial).
- `baseColor` / `outlineColor`: Custom colors for radial displays.

## Best Practices
- Limit slices to 5-7 to maintain readability. [Source: piechart.adoc]
- Order slices by size, starting with the largest at 12 o'clock.
- Use donut style for a more modern appearance.
- Avoid using pie charts for time-series trends; use line or bar charts instead.

## Backlinks
- [[charts-and-metrics]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Wiki Log
nodes.description: Chronological record of knowledge base updates.
nodes.content:

## [2026-04-25] ingest | Ingested Navigation & Organization details (3 pages)

## [2026-04-27] update | Fixed Python dependency issue by implementing automated venv management in publish_wiki.sh

nodes.label: Domain Visualizer
nodes.description: Domain visualizations map business or technical architectures into clean, hierarchical diagrams.
nodes.content:
Domain visualizations map business or technical architectures into clean, hierarchical diagrams.

## Key Features
- **Hierarchical Layout**: Organizes content into main nodes, common capabilities, and specialized groups. [Source: domain.adoc]
- **Wiki-Style Links**: Transform nodes into clickable links to documentation or repositories. [Source: domain.adoc]
- **Emoji Support**: Integrated visual icons for quick identification of verticals. [Source: domain.adoc]
- **Auto-Layout**: Smart positioning prevents node and path overlaps. [Source: domain.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `main`: Declares the root node of the domain. [Source: domain.adoc]
- `type`: `COMMON` for shared foundations or custom names for specialized verticals.
- `useNeural=true`: Alternative neural network visual style.
- `useDark=true`: Enables dark theme.

## Best Practices
- Start by declaring the `main` node first. [Source: domain.adoc]
- Group infrastructure and shared services in `COMMON` rows.
- Limit to 2-3 rows per group to maintain readability. [Source: domain.adoc]
- Use emojis sparingly (e.g., one per vertical) for visual distinction.
- Keep node names short (1-2 words) and link to detailed documentation.

## Backlinks
- [[technical-and-architecture]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Wiki Index
nodes.description: Central index of all knowledge base pages.
nodes.content:
# DocOps Extension Server Wiki Index

Central index of all knowledge base pages.

## Core Concepts
- [[docops-extension-server]]: Primary server for documentation extensions.
- [[visualizations]]: Overview of all visualization types.
- [[asciidoctor-support]]: Integration with AsciiDoctor.
- [[realtime-editor]]: Live editing tool for DocOps visualizations.
- [[showcase-for-visuals]]: Demonstration of visualization types.

## Visualization Categories
- [[charts-and-metrics]]: Data-driven visualizations like bar and pie charts.
  - [[barchart]]: Category comparisons and rankings.
  - [[piechart]]: Proportional data and market share.
  - [[linechart]]: Trends over time and multi-series tracking.
  - [[combination]]: Mixed chart types with dual Y-axes.
  - [[gaugechart]]: Single metric status and speedometers.
  - [[metricscard]]: KPI snapshots and executive summaries.
- [[navigation-and-organization]]: Structural elements like timelines and scorecards.
  - [[scorecard]]: Before/after comparisons and release summaries.
  - [[timeline]]: Chronological narratives and project roadmaps.
  - [[buttons]]: Navigation grids and resource directories.
- [[process-and-planning]]: Workflow and priority mapping tools.
  - [[callout]]: Highlighted processes and key metrics.
  - [[planner]]: Kanban roadmaps and sprint planning.
  - [[connectors]]: Sequential workflow arrows.
  - [[releasestrategy]]: Milestone timelines and release plans.
  - [[quadrant]]: 2D priority mapping and risk assessment.
- [[technical-and-architecture]]: System design and hierarchy visualizations.
  - [[wordcloud]]: Text hierarchies and keyword analysis.
  - [[domain]]: Architecture maps and system landscapes.
  - [[gherkin]]: BDD test scenario visualization.
  - [[treechart]]: Hierarchical diagrams and org charts.
  - [[treemap]]: Proportional resource distribution.
- [[metadata-and-status]]: Tracking decisions, tasks, and indicators.
  - [[shield]]: Status indicators, badges, and metadata tags.
  - [[placemat]]: Color-coded label grids for component inventories.
  - [[todo]]: Task tracking with Kanban, list, and grid layouts.
  - [[vcard]]: Professional contact cards with QR code generation.
  - [[adr]]: Architectural Decision Records with context and consequences.

## Source Summaries
- [[index-adoc-summary]]: Summary of the main AsciiDoctor index page.

nodes.label: DocOps Steps
nodes.description: {#top}
nodes.content:
{#top}

# DocOps Steps

## Introduction

*Create beautiful isometric step infographics to visualize processes, workflows, and progress*

**Key features:**

* Isometric 3D step visualization with numbered stages
* Configurable themes and color palettes
* CSV-style data format with header row for easy authoring
* Support for custom colors per step
* Adjustable canvas size, layout offsets, and label positioning

## Default Example

Basic isometric steps showing enterprise highlights:

```asciidoc
[docops,steps,controls=true]
----
infographic=isometric-steps
title=Enterprise Advantage Highlights
subtitle=Showcasing core strengths and performance across key dimensions
---
Order | Title | Description | Color
1 | Brand Influence | Widely recognized and trusted by customers | #6EAEFF
2 | R&D Strength | Strong in-house innovation capabilities | #69DEE5
3 | Rapid Market Growth | Users and revenue grew quickly in one year | #F8BC95
4 | Service Excellence | High satisfaction and service quality scores | #D9AEF8
5 | Data Security | Robust protection and compliance systems | #B0A5FB
6 | Innovation Leadership | Advanced product and technology roadmap | #A7E58A
----
```

[Back to top](#top)

## Focused Examples

### Software Development Lifecycle

```asciidoc
[docops,step,controls=true]
----
infographic=isometric-steps
version=1
title=Software Development Lifecycle
subtitle=Key phases from planning to deployment
canvasWidth=1220
canvasHeight=760
theme=ayu
palette=vibrant
---
Order | Title | Description | Color
1 | Planning | Define requirements and project scope | #6EAEFF
2 | Design | Architect the solution and create specs | #69DEE5
3 | Development | Write and review code | #F8BC95
4 | Testing | Validate quality and fix defects | #D9AEF8
5 | Deployment | Release to production environment | #A7E58A
----
```

### Dark Theme Version

```asciidoc
[docops,steps,controls=true,useDark=true]
----
infographic=isometric-steps
version=1
title=Customer Onboarding Journey
subtitle=Steps to welcome and activate new customers
canvasWidth=1220
canvasHeight=760
theme=ayu
palette=vibrant
---
Order | Title | Description | Color
1 | Sign Up | Customer creates an account | #6EAEFF
2 | Verification | Identity and email verification | #69DEE5
3 | Profile Setup | Complete preferences and settings | #F8BC95
4 | First Use | Guided walkthrough of key features | #D9AEF8
5 | Engagement | Regular usage and feedback loop | #B0A5FB
----
```

### Road View

Use `view=road` to switch from the default isometric blocks to a winding road layout with connected step cards:

```asciidoc
[docops,steps,controls=true]
----
infographic=isometric-steps
version=1
title=Product Launch Roadmap
subtitle=From idea to market
canvasWidth=1220
canvasHeight=760
view=road
theme=ayu
palette=vibrant
---
Order | Title | Description | Color
1 | Ideation | Brainstorm and validate the concept | #6EAEFF
2 | Prototype | Build a working proof of concept | #69DEE5
3 | Beta Test | Gather feedback from early users | #F8BC95
4 | Refinement | Polish based on user insights | #D9AEF8
5 | Launch | Release to the public | #A7E58A
----
```

### Minimal Three-Step Process

```asciidoc
[docops,steps,controls=true]
----
infographic=isometric-steps
version=1
title=Quick Start Guide
canvasWidth=1220
canvasHeight=760
theme=classic
palette=vibrant
---
Order | Title | Description
1 | Install | Download and install the application
2 | Configure | Set up your preferences
3 | Launch | Start using the product
----
```

[Back to top](#top)

## Common Use Cases

* *Process documentation* - Visualize workflows and business processes
* *Onboarding guides* - Show step-by-step user or employee onboarding
* *Project milestones* - Display key phases of a project lifecycle
* *Training materials* - Break down learning paths into clear stages
* *Product roadmaps* - Illustrate feature development progression

[Back to top](#top)

## Format Options

### Basic Structure

```asciidoc
[docops,steps,controls=true]
----
infographic=isometric-steps
version=1
title=My Steps Title
subtitle=Optional subtitle text
canvasWidth=1220
canvasHeight=760
theme=classic
palette=vibrant
---
Order | Title | Description | Color
1 | Step One | Description of step one | #6EAEFF
2 | Step Two | Description of step two | #69DEE5
----
```

### Configuration Fields

| Field       | Default      | Purpose                                                                 |
|-------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| infographic | _(required)_ | Type of infographic (use `isometric-steps`)                             |
| version     | `1`          | Format version                                                          |
| title       | _(required)_ | Main title displayed on the infographic                                 |
| subtitle    | _(none)_     | Optional subtitle below the title                                       |
| view        | `isometric`  | View style: `isometric` (default 3D blocks) or `road` (winding road layout) |
| canvasWidth | `1220`       | Width of the SVG canvas in pixels                                       |
| canvasHeight| `760`        | Height of the SVG canvas in pixels                                      |
| theme       | `classic`    | Visual theme name (e.g., `classic`, `brand`)                            |
| palette     | `vibrant`    | Color palette for step rendering                                        |
| startX      | _(auto)_     | Custom X starting position                                              |
| startY      | _(auto)_     | Custom Y starting position                                              |
| dx          | _(auto)_     | Horizontal offset between steps                                         |
| dy          | _(auto)_     | Vertical offset between steps                                           |
| labelOffsetX| `130`        | Horizontal offset for step labels                                       |
| labelOffsetY| `65`         | Vertical offset for step labels                                         |
| maxDescLines| `2`          | Maximum description lines per step                                      |

### Step Data Columns

| Column     | Required | Purpose                                   |
|------------|----------|-------------------------------------------|
| Order      | Yes      | Numeric order of the step (must be unique)|
| Title      | Yes      | Short title for the step                  |
| Description| No       | Brief description text                    |
| Color      | No       | Custom hex color (e.g., `#6EAEFF`)        |

### Data Format Rules

* Config section and data section are separated by `---`
* First row after `---` is the header row
* Columns are separated by `|` (pipe)
* Order values must be unique integers
* Color is optional; palette colors are used when omitted

Example:

```
infographic=isometric-steps
version=1
title=My Process
---
Order | Title | Description | Color
1 | First Step | Getting started | #6EAEFF
2 | Second Step | Making progress | #69DEE5
3 | Final Step | Completing the task | #A7E58A
```

[Back to top](#top)

## Best Practices

* *Keep titles short* - Use 2-3 words per step for best visual results
* *Limit to 3-8 steps* - Too many steps reduce readability
* *Use consistent colors* - Stick to a palette or let the theme handle it
* *Write concise descriptions* - One line per step works best
* *Order sequentially* - Use 1, 2, 3... for clear progression
* *Choose meaningful subtitles* - They provide context for the entire infographic

> **TIP**  
> For best results, keep step titles short and action-oriented. Use the description field for additional context, and let the color palette create visual harmony unless you need specific brand colors.

[Back to top](#top)

nodes.label: Charts and Metrics
nodes.description: Visualization category focused on data representation, KPIs, and trend analysis.
nodes.content:
Visualization category focused on data representation, KPIs, and trend analysis.

## Description
This category includes 6 visualization types designed for category comparisons, proportional data, and single metric snapshots. [Source: index.adoc]

## Visualization Types
- **[[barchart]]**: Category comparisons and rankings.
- **[[piechart]]**: Proportional data and market share.
- **[[linechart]]**: Trends over time and multi-series tracking.
- **[[combination]]**: Mixed chart types with dual Y-axes.
- **[[gaugechart]]**: Single metric status and Speedometers.
- **[[metricscard]]**: KPI snapshots and executive summaries.

## Backlinks
- [[index]]
- [[visualizations]]
- [[index-adoc-summary]]

nodes.label: Release Strategy
nodes.description: Release strategies visualize product release plans and milestone roadmaps.
nodes.content:
Release strategies visualize product release plans and milestone roadmaps.

## Key Features
- **2 Layout Styles**: `TLS` for condensed timeline views and `R` for detailed roadmap layouts. [Source: releasestrategy.adoc]
- **Milestone Details**: Support for release types (M1, RC1, GA), goals, and activity lists. [Source: releasestrategy.adoc]
- **Modern Design**: Glass morphism with translucent depth effects. [Source: releasestrategy.adoc]
- **Custom Palettes**: Supports specific color palettes for brand alignment. [Source: releasestrategy.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `style`: `TLS` (Timeline Summary) or `R` (Roadmap).
- `releases`: Array of release objects containing `type`, `date`, `goal`, and `lines` (activities).
- `displayConfig.colors`: Array of 3 hex colors for the theme.
- `useDark=true`: Enables dark theme.

## Best Practices
- Use semantic release types (M1 for milestones, GA for general availability). [Source: releasestrategy.adoc]
- Keep activity lists focused to 3-5 items per release. [Source: releasestrategy.adoc]
- Use `R` style for multi-year or detailed quarterly plans.
- Scale up (e.g., `1.5`) for presentations and down (e.g., `0.7`) for embedded docs.

## Backlinks
- [[process-and-planning]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Wiki Health Check Report - 2026-04-25
nodes.description: No errors found.
nodes.content:
# Wiki Health Check Report - 2026-04-25

## 🔴 Errors
No errors found.

## 🟡 Warnings
- Orphan page: [[lint-report-2026-04-25]] has no inbound links.

## 🔵 Info
- Low connectivity: [[lint-report-2026-04-25]] has no outgoing internal links.

## Knowledge Gap Analysis
The following areas represent the biggest gaps in the current knowledge base:

1. **Markdown Visualization Parity**: While AsciiDoctor visuals are well-documented, the Markdown counterparts in `src/docs/md/` are not yet ingested.
2. **Architectural Deep Dives**: Core system internals (`arch.adoc`, `parsing.adoc`) are missing, leaving a gap in understanding how the server actually processes data.
3. **Operational Guides**: Tutorials and showcases are not yet represented, which limits the wiki's utility for end-users seeking implementation examples.

### Suggested Next Articles
- **DocOps Markdown Ecosystem** (`src/docs/md/index.md`): To establish the second pillar of the visualization suite.
- **System Architecture** (`src/docs/asciidoc/arch.adoc`): To provide technical depth to the existing entity pages.
- **Master Showcase** (`src/docs/asciidoc/showcase.adoc`): To link abstract concepts to concrete visual examples.

nodes.label: Gherkin
nodes.description: Gherkin visualizations transform BDD test scenarios into visual Given-When-Then formats.
nodes.content:
Gherkin visualizations transform BDD test scenarios into visual Given-When-Then formats.

## Key Features
- **Format Support**: Accepts plain Gherkin text or JSON formats. [Source: gherkin.adoc]
- **Status Indicators**: Visual cues for `PASSING`, `FAILING`, `PENDING`, and `SKIPPED` steps. [Source: gherkin.adoc]
- **Atomic Icons**: Distinct icons for Features, Scenarios, and individual Steps. [Source: gherkin.adoc]
- **Custom Themes**: Full control over colors, layout, and typography via JSON. [Source: gherkin.adoc]

## Step Keywords
- **Given**: Initial context or preconditions (Blue).
- **When**: Action or event triggering behavior (Orange).
- **Then**: Expected outcome or assertion (Green).
- **And** / **But**: Chain related steps or exceptions (Gray).

## Best Practices
- Keep steps atomic (one action or assertion per step). [Source: gherkin.adoc]
- Use `AND` for lists to avoid repeating keywords.
- Include both happy path and edge cases in documentation.
- Embed `.feature` files directly using `include::` to maintain a single source of truth. [Source: gherkin.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[technical-and-architecture]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: DocOps Extension Server
nodes.description: The DocOps Extension Server is a system designed to provide advanced visualization capabilities for documentation.
nodes.content:
The DocOps Extension Server is a system designed to provide advanced visualization capabilities for documentation.

## Overview
It supports multiple formats including AsciiDoctor and Markdown. It enables documentation writers to embed complex visual elements like charts, timelines, and architecture maps using simple JSON-based configurations. [Source: index.adoc]

## Integration
- **AsciiDoctor**: Deep integration via custom blocks like `[docops]`.
- **Markdown**: Supported as an alternative documentation format.

## Backlinks
- [[index]]
- [[index-adoc-summary]]

nodes.label: Planner
nodes.description: Planners organize tasks into a Kanban-style visualization with now, next, later, and done columns.
nodes.content:
Planners organize tasks into a Kanban-style visualization with now, next, later, and done columns.

## Key Features
- **4-Column Layout**: Organizes work into `now`, `next`, `later`, and `done` stages. [Source: planner.adoc]
- **Task Cards**: Supports title and multi-line descriptions for each task. [Source: planner.adoc]
- **Visual Distinction**: Color-coded columns for instant stage recognition. [Source: planner.adoc]
- **Glass Design**: Modern, layered appearance for roadmaps and plans. [Source: planner.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `title`: Header for the planner.
- `useDark=true`: Enables dark theme.
- **Stage Keywords**: Tasks must start with `- now`, `- next`, `- later`, or `- done`. [Source: planner.adoc]

## Best Practices
- Limit **NOW** to 2-4 items to maintain focus. [Source: planner.adoc]
- Use **NEXT** for the immediate queue (3-5 items).
- Use **DONE** to celebrate recent progress and velocity. [Source: planner.adoc]
- Keep titles to one line; use descriptions for details.

## Backlinks
- [[process-and-planning]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Tree Charts
nodes.description: Tree charts provide hierarchical diagrams for organizational structures, taxonomies, and decision trees.
nodes.content:
Tree charts provide hierarchical diagrams for organizational structures, taxonomies, and decision trees.

## Key Features
- **Layout Orientations**: Supports both vertical (top-down) and horizontal (left-right) layouts. [Source: treechart.adoc]
- **Indentation Syntax**: Simple indentation-based data format for easy authoring. [Source: treechart.adoc]
- **Interactive Nodes**: Collapsible nodes for managing large, complex hierarchies. [Source: treechart.adoc]
- **Glass Effect**: Modern visual style with translucent node backgrounds. [Source: treechart.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `orientation`: "vertical" or "horizontal".
- `collapsible`: Enable or disable interactive node expansion.
- `colors`: Define a custom palette of hex colors.
- `width` / `height`: Canvas dimensions.

## Best Practices
- Keep tree depth to 4-5 levels for better readability. [Source: treechart.adoc]
- Use horizontal orientation for "wide" trees with many branches at each level.
- Set `collapsible=false` for PDF exports to ensure all content is visible.
- Color-code nodes by category (e.g., Department or Skill) for visual distinction.

## Backlinks
- [[technical-and-architecture]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Gauge Charts
nodes.description: Gauge charts turn single metrics into visual speedometers, providing instant status feedback.
nodes.content:
Gauge charts turn single metrics into visual speedometers, providing instant status feedback.

## Key Features
- **Variety**: Supports 7 types including `SEMI_CIRCLE`, `DIGITAL`, `LINEAR`, and `MULTI_GAUGE`. [Source: gaugechart.adoc]
- **Status Zones**: Automatic color-coding for Normal (Green), Caution (Yellow), and Critical (Red) ranges. [Source: gaugechart.adoc]
- **Grid Layouts**: `MULTI_GAUGE` allows monitoring multiple metrics in a compact grid. [Source: gaugechart.adoc]

## Gauge Types
- `SEMI_CIRCLE`: Classic performance indicators (CPU, memory).
- `DIGITAL`: Prominent single metrics requiring attention.
- `LINEAR`: Horizontal progress bars with target lines.
- `MULTI_GAUGE`: Grids of 3-9 gauges for fleet monitoring.

## Configuration Options
- `showRanges=true`: Displays color zones (default).
- `showTarget=true`: Adds goal lines (for `LINEAR` type).
- `columns`: Defines grid width for multi-gauge displays.

## Best Practices
- Reserve `DIGITAL` for "hero metrics" that demand immediate focus.
- Use `MULTI_GAUGE` for comparing 3-9 related metrics side-by-side. [Source: gaugechart.adoc]
- Set realistic min/max bounds based on actual operating limits.
- Keep units consistent across all gauges in a grid.

## Backlinks
- [[charts-and-metrics]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Line Charts
nodes.description: Line charts reveal trends over time, making patterns, peaks, and valleys immediately visible.
nodes.content:
Line charts reveal trends over time, making patterns, peaks, and valleys immediately visible.

## Key Features
- **Multi-Series**: Supports up to 6 related trends side-by-side. [Source: linechart.adoc]
- **Visual Styles**: Smooth (curved) or angular lines to fit the data type. [Source: linechart.adoc]
- **Area Fill**: Optional area shading under the curve to emphasize volume. [Source: linechart.adoc]
- **Auto-Scaling**: Smart axis ranges calculated based on provided data. [Source: linechart.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `smoothLines=true`: Enables curved lines (good for volatile data trends).
- `showArea=true`: Fills the area under the lines.
- `width`: Customizable chart width.

## Best Practices
- Use consistent time intervals (e.g., don't skip months).
- Limit to 4-6 series to prevent visual confusion. [Source: linechart.adoc]
- Use smooth lines for overall trends and angular lines for exact data points.
- Start the Y-axis at zero unless showing small variations in large numbers.

## Backlinks
- [[charts-and-metrics]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Architectural Decision Records (ADR)
nodes.description: Architectural Decision Records (ADR) document key technical choices along with their context, rationale, and consequences.
nodes.content:
Architectural Decision Records (ADR) document key technical choices along with their context, rationale, and consequences.

## Key Features
- **Structured Format**: Captures `Context`, `Decision`, `Consequences`, and `Participants`. [Source: adr.adoc]
- **Status Tracking**: Visualizes the state of a decision (e.g., `Proposed`, `Accepted`, `Superseded`). [Source: adr.adoc]
- **Participant Visualization**: Shows the people involved in the decision, including their roles and contact info. [Source: adr.adoc]
- **Wiki-Style Links**: Enables linking to external references or related documentation. [Source: adr.adoc]

## Decision Statuses
- **Proposed**: Decision being considered.
- **Accepted**: Decision approved and active.
- **Superseded**: Replaced by a newer decision.
- **Deprecated**: No longer relevant.
- **Rejected**: Decision not approved.

## Best Practices
- Keep context concise (3-5 bullet points) to explain "why now". [Source: adr.adoc]
- Make decisions actionable and definitive.
- List both positive and negative outcomes in the consequences section. [Source: adr.adoc]
- Use the status field to maintain a clear history of decision evolution; don't just edit old records. [Source: adr.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[metadata-and-status]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Quadrant Charts
nodes.description: Quadrant charts provide 2D priority mapping for strategic decision-making and risk analysis.
nodes.content:
Quadrant charts provide 2D priority mapping for strategic decision-making and risk analysis.

## Key Features
- **4-Quadrant Grid**: Best for impact vs. effort or business value vs. complexity mapping. [Source: quadrant.adoc]
- **Custom Labels**: Full control over axis and individual quadrant titles. [Source: quadrant.adoc]
- **Categorization**: Color-code data points by category (e.g., by team or priority). [Source: quadrant.adoc]
- **Visual Version 2**: Enhanced modern design for better clarity. [Source: quadrant.adoc]

## Standard Quadrant Meaning (Signal Profile)
- **Leaders**: Top-right (High Impact, High Effort) - "Big Bets". [Source: quadrant.adoc]
- **Challengers**: Top-left (High Impact, Low Effort) - "Quick Wins". [Source: quadrant.adoc]
- **Visionaries**: Bottom-left (Low Impact, Low Effort) - "Fill-ins". [Source: quadrant.adoc]
- **Niche**: Bottom-right (Low Impact, High Effort) - "Avoid/Thankless". [Source: quadrant.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `xAxis` / `yAxis`: Customizable labels for the grid axes.
- `leaders` / `challengers` / `visionaries` / `niche`: Custom labels for each quadrant.
- `visualVersion=2`: Modern design style.

## Best Practices
- Label quadrants contextually (e.g., "Quick Wins") rather than using generic terms.
- Limit to 8-12 items to prevent visual overlap and clutter. [Source: quadrant.adoc]
- Match axes to the specific analysis (e.g., Probability vs. Impact for risks). [Source: quadrant.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[process-and-planning]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Realtime Editor
nodes.description: A tool for live editing and previewing DocOps visualizations.
nodes.content:
A tool for live editing and previewing DocOps visualizations.

## Description
The Realtime Editor allows users to see changes to their DocOps configurations in real-time. It is linked from the main documentation index. [Source: index.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[index-adoc-summary]]
- [[docops-extension-server]]

nodes.label: Treemaps
nodes.description: Treemaps use proportional rectangles to visualize budget allocations, market share, and resource distribution.
nodes.content:
Treemaps use proportional rectangles to visualize budget allocations, market share, and resource distribution.

## Key Features
- **Nested Rectangles**: Rectangles are sized based on their relative numeric values. [Source: treemap.adoc]
- **Proportional Sizing**: Makes it easy to see large vs. small contributors in a hierarchy. [Source: treemap.adoc]
- **Multiple Themes**: Supports `modern` and `brutalist` (high-contrast) visual styles. [Source: treemap.adoc]
- **Auto-Labels**: Automatic display of percentages and values within each rectangle. [Source: treemap.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `theme`: `modern` (default) or `brutalist`.
- `paletteType`: Color schemes like `OCEAN_BREEZE`, `SUNSET`, or `PASTEL`.
- `showValues` / `showPercentages`: Toggle numeric data visibility.
- `width` / `height`: Customizable dimensions (default 1200x800).

## Best Practices
- Limit to 6-10 main categories; too many rectangles reduce readability. [Source: treemap.adoc]
- Use descriptive subtitles to clarify the data context.
- Format metric values (e.g., "$2.4M" vs "2400000") for clarity. [Source: treemap.adoc]
- Group very small items into an "Other" category to keep the map focused. [Source: treemap.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[technical-and-architecture]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: AsciiDoctor Support
nodes.description: Integration details for using DocOps extensions within AsciiDoctor documents.
nodes.content:
Integration details for using DocOps extensions within AsciiDoctor documents.

## Implementation
Visualizations are integrated into AsciiDoctor using custom extension blocks (e.g., `[docops,buttons]`). Configurations are provided as JSON objects within these blocks. [Source: index.adoc]

## Key Features
- **Conditional Rendering**: Uses `ifdef::backend-html5[]` to ensure visualizations are rendered in HTML outputs.
- **Custom Attributes**: Supports attributes like `:icons:`, `:stylesdir:`, and `:stylesheet:`. [Source: index.adoc]

## Backlinks
- [[index]]
- [[docops-extension-server]]

nodes.label: Badges & Shields
nodes.description: Badges and shields add visual metadata to documentation, providing compact and scannable indicators for status, versions, or metrics.
nodes.content:
Badges and shields add visual metadata to documentation, providing compact and scannable indicators for status, versions, or metrics.

## Key Features
- **7 Visual Styles**: Includes `classic`, `glassmorphic`, `neon`, `brutalist`, `gradient`, `minimal`, and `neumorphic`. [Source: shield.adoc]
- **Icon Support**: Embed logos from the SimpleIcons library (1,000+ icons available). [Source: shield.adoc]
- **Flexible Layouts**: Arrange badges in `HORIZONTAL`, `VERTICAL`, or `GRID` layouts. [Source: shield.adoc]
- **Interactive**: Badges can be made clickable to link directly to builds, releases, or documentation. [Source: shield.adoc]

## Configuration Options
- `type`: Selects the visual style (e.g., `glass`).
- `arrangement`: Defines the layout (Horizontal/Vertical/Grid).
- `spacing`: Adjusts pixel space between badges.
- **Pipe-Delimited Data**: `Label|Message|URL|LabelColor|MessageColor|Logo|FontColor`. [Source: shield.adoc]

## Best Practices
- Use icons (e.g., `<kotlin>`, `<docker>`) for instant recognition. [Source: shield.adoc]
- Color-code by meaning (Green for success, Red for failure, Yellow for warning).
- Keep labels short (1-2 words) to maintain scanability.
- Group related badges into a grid to create status dashboards.

## Backlinks
- [[metadata-and-status]]
- [[visualizations]]

nodes.label: Metadata and Status
nodes.description: Visualization category focused on tracking decisions, tasks, and team information.
nodes.content:
Visualization category focused on tracking decisions, tasks, and team information.

## Description
This category includes 5 types for status indicators, label grids, task lists, and contact information. [Source: index.adoc]

## Visualization Types
- **[[shield]]**: Status indicators, badges, and metadata tags.
- **[[placemat]]**: Color-coded label grids for component inventories.
- **[[todo]]**: Task tracking with Kanban, list, and grid layouts.
- **[[vcard]]**: Professional contact cards with QR code generation.
- **[[adr]]**: Architectural Decision Records with context and consequences.

## Backlinks
- [[index]]
- [[visualizations]]
- [[index-adoc-summary]]
